_meta.source: FMP MCP connector (quote endpoint for VIX; premarket/news scan for flags)
_meta.note: Companion file for the dip/pop OHLCV feed (dip_pop_ohlcv.csv). Overwritten each run (not appended) — always reflects the latest snapshot, refreshed at premarket and market-close task runs. Flag values: Normal | Caution | Avoid. Avoid should gate a green technical verdict; Caution should annotate it.
_meta.category_note: category = the primary driver behind the flag: macro-scheduled | sector-contagion | single-name | geopolitical | other. See dip_pop_flag_history.csv for the append-only burn-in log.

flags.reason: Memory rout continues (day 3): MU -5.65% ($853.20 close) as TSMC's blowout Q2 (record profit, +33% rev, raised US capex to $100B) triggered capex-fear selloff across the AI/semi complex instead of relief (Investopedia, Zacks, Jul 16); broad chip names down 2nd-3rd straight session

SNDK -12.6% ($1,411.08 close), steepest single-day drop of the current memory rout; Motley Fool (Jul 16) still bullish long-term NAND-shortage thesis (pricier iPhone memory), but today's move tracks the broader MU/AVGO/FSLR complex selloff on TSM capex-fear contagion, not SNDK-specific news; beta 4.74, treat with care

MSFT +1.4% ($401.10 close) bucking the Nasdaq's -1.3% day; stronger jobless claims (208K vs 220K exp, Dept of Labor) + blowout Philly Fed (41.4 vs 13 exp, Jul 16) offset chip-sector drag; session high $405.99 retested the $396 former range top for a 2nd session (Position Tracker breakout watch)
